texts.hearing_script:
Case: live_in_aide_case_effective_accommodation_001
Requested remedy: [Maintain the effective separate-bedroom accommodation for the live-in aide]
Requesting party: [Requesting Party]

Opening:
This case asks whether the approved live-in aide accommodation is effective in practice.
The current evaluated record reflects that the provider recognized the accommodation duty and implemented an arrangement that appears effective in practice.
Citations: findings=dutyToGrant, constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=none; evidence=none; events=none

Core points:
- No functional harms were established on the accepted findings.
- Supporting authorities for the accommodation duty include: Giebeler v. M&B Associates, United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., McGary v. City of Portland, 24 CFR 982.316.

Closing:
On the accepted findings, this record looks more like a successfully implemented accommodation than a denial.
texts.complaint_outline:
Case: live_in_aide_case_effective_accommodation_001
Requested remedy: [Maintain the effective separate-bedroom accommodation for the live-in aide]
Requesting party: [Requesting Party]

Complaint Outline For live_in_aide_case_effective_accommodation_001

Claim:
Failure to provide a reasonable accommodation under the Fair Housing Act.
Citations: findings=necessary, reasonable, constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=none; evidence=none; events=none

Core allegations:
- The tenant is disabled and needs a live-in aide.
- Medical verification supports the requested accommodation.
- A separate bedroom was requested for the aide.
- The housing provider approved the aide in principle and the current record indicates the separate-bedroom request was satisfied in practice.
- No functional harms were established on the accepted findings.

Theory:
The accepted findings currently point away from a denial theory because the requested accommodation appears to have been satisfied in effective form.

Authorities to cite: Giebeler v. M&B Associates, United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., McGary v. City of Portland, 24 CFR 982.316.
texts.demand_letter:
Subject: Request to Preserve Effective Live-In Aide Accommodation

To: [Housing Authority or Decision-Maker]

From: [Requesting Party]
Requested remedy: [Maintain the effective separate-bedroom accommodation for the live-in aide]
Requested response deadline: [10 business days]

This letter requests prompt approval of an accommodation that is effective in practice, not merely nominal.
The current evaluated record suggests the accommodation has been implemented in a workable form, so the immediate focus should be preserving that arrangement rather than escalating a denial claim.
Citations: findings=constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=none; evidence=none; events=none
The present reasoning output identifies these harms: No functional harms were established on the accepted findings.
Supporting authorities include Giebeler v. M&B Associates, United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., McGary v. City of Portland, 24 CFR 982.316.

Please confirm in writing that the current separate-bedroom arrangement for the live-in aide will be maintained.
Please preserve the current arrangement and identify any future changes early enough for the household to respond without disruption.

Sincerely,
[Requesting Party]
